description: Implement the TaskStore interface and the JSONL/file-based backend. The store separates task reading (immutable) from lifecycle management (mutable, with locking). The JSONL store reads tasks from a configurable directory and stores lifecycle records as individual JSON files with file-based locking. ClaimNextReady must be atomic: lock, verify status, update, release. Use optimistic concurrency via the Version field. Reference docs/implementation-plan.md Milestone 2 for the full interface, error types, and JSONL implementation details.
commit: feat(store): add TaskStore interface and JSONL file-based implementation
steps: Create internal/store/store.go with the TaskStore interface (ListTasks, GetTask, CreateLifecycle, GetLifecycle, UpdateLifecycle, ListLifecycles, ClaimNextReady), TaskFilter, and LifecycleFilter structs matching docs/implementation-plan.md Milestone 2a
Create internal/store/errors.go with sentinel errors: ErrTaskNotFound, ErrNoReadyTasks, ErrLifecycleExists, ErrStaleWrite
Add github.com/gofrs/flock dependency for cross-platform file locking

Create internal/store/jsonl/store.go: implement the JSONL store. Tasks loaded from directory using task.ParseDir. Lifecycles stored as pretty-printed JSON in lifecycle directory. File locking via flock for concurrency. ClaimNextReady: list lifecycle files, sort by priority (join with tasks), try to flock each ready lifecycle, verify still ready, update to running, write, release. UpdateLifecycle checks Version field for stale write detection.

Create internal/store/jsonl/store_test.go: test ListTasks with fixture files, test CreateLifecycle+GetLifecycle round-trip, test UpdateLifecycle with stale write detection, test ClaimNextReady returns highest priority, test ClaimNextReady with no ready tasks, test concurrent ClaimNextReady with goroutines verifying no double-claim
acceptance_criteria: TaskStore interface matches docs/implementation-plan.md Milestone 2a exactly
All four sentinel errors are defined
JSONL store loads tasks from a directory of .json files
Lifecycle files are pretty-printed JSON for human readability
ClaimNextReady atomically claims the highest-priority ready task
ClaimNextReady returns ErrNoReadyTasks when none are available
UpdateLifecycle returns ErrStaleWrite when Version doesn't match
Concurrent ClaimNextReady calls never double-claim a task
